subject: MATHEMATICS
title: Numbers in Symbols and Words
objective: Connect standard, expanded, and word forms.
introduction: Different forms can represent the same whole number. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Numbers in Symbols and Words”.
A key word used in the lesson “Numbers in Symbols and Words”.
A key word used in the lesson “Numbers in Symbols and Words”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Different forms can represent the same whole number.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Different forms can represent the same whole number.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. two hundred forty-eight and twenty thousand forty-eight do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Numbers in Symbols and Words”?

options.text: Different forms can represent the same whole number.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Different forms can represent the same whole number.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Different forms can represent the same whole number.
The correct response is “2,048” because it follows the lesson explanation: Different forms can represent the same whole number.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “two hundred forty-eight” because it follows the lesson explanation: Different forms can represent the same whole number.
The correct response is “two thousand forty-eight” because it follows the lesson explanation: Different forms can represent the same whole number.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “two thousand forty-eight” because it follows the lesson explanation: Different forms can represent the same whole number.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Numbers in Symbols and Words; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Numbers in Symbols and Words.
